items.prompt: A function returns a newly allocated reversed copy of an array of length n and otherwise uses only two indices. Select its time, auxiliary-space, and output-space complexity.
constraints: The returned reversed array contains n values.

feedback.reason: The algorithm reads n values, uses constant working state, and materializes an n-element result.
feedback.details: The returned copy is output space rather than auxiliary memory. The two indices remain O(1) auxiliary state.

items.prompt: A function reverses an array in place using left, right, and one temporary value, then returns the same array reference. Select its time, auxiliary-space, and additional output-space complexity.
constraints: No new result array is allocated.

feedback.reason: In-place reversal performs a linear number of swaps and reuses the input storage.
feedback.details: The n-element input still exists, but it is not auxiliary memory. Only constant local state is added, and no growing output container is allocated.

items.prompt: A function must return every distinct value. It builds a set of distinct values and then copies those values into a separate result array. If there are d distinct values, how should the two structures be classified?

options.text: The set is O(d) auxiliary space and the returned array is O(d) output space.
Both structures are output space because they contain the same values.
Both structures are auxiliary space because they are created during execution.
The set is output space and the returned array is auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: Classification follows each structure's role, not whether it contains similar data.
feedback.details: The set is temporary state used to construct the required result. The separate returned array is the output. Both scale with d, but they belong to different accounting categories.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_output: The temporary set is not returned or otherwise part of the required result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_auxiliary: The returned array is required output and should not be counted as temporary working memory.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.set_output_array_aux: This reverses the actual roles of the two structures.

items.prompt: A function builds a map of value frequencies and returns that same map as its required result. It uses no other growing structure. Which classification is most precise?

options.text: The map is O(d) output space; auxiliary space outside the returned map is O(1).
The map is O(d) auxiliary space and output space is O(1).
The map is both O(d) output space and O(d) auxiliary space.
Both output and auxiliary space are O(1) because each update is constant time.

feedback.reason: A structure created during execution can still be output space when it is the required returned result.
feedback.details: The map stores one entry per distinct value and is returned directly. It therefore represents O(d) output storage. Constant-time updates do not imply constant memory.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.map_is_auxiliary: The map is not temporary; it is the required result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.map_counts_twice: One structure should not be counted in both categories merely because it was built incrementally.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_constant: Per-operation time says nothing about the final number of stored entries.

items.prompt: A function returns the prefix-sum array for an input of length n and writes each prefix sum directly into the returned array. It uses one running total. Which statement is correct?

options.text: O(n) output space and O(1) auxiliary space.
O(1) output space and O(n) auxiliary space.
O(n) output space and O(n) auxiliary space because the result is built incrementally.
O(1) output space and O(1) auxiliary space because only one pass is used.

feedback.reason: The returned prefix array is output, while the running total is constant working state.
feedback.details: The algorithm materializes n result values. Building the result one element at a time does not turn that required output into auxiliary space.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.output_1_aux_n: The n-element returned array is output, not auxiliary memory.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_n: The output array should not also be charged as temporary state.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_1: One pass does not make an n-element result constant-size.

items.prompt: A function normalizes two inputs of lengths n and m into temporary buffers of retained lengths k and r, compares them, and returns one boolean. Select its time, auxiliary-space, and output-space complexity.
constraints: Each input is normalized in one pass.

feedback.reason: The normalized copies are temporary working state, while the required result is only one boolean.
feedback.details: Both original inputs are scanned, the buffers occupy O(k + r) auxiliary space, and the constant-size boolean is output.

items.prompt: Arrange the steps for analyzing the space usage of a sequence algorithm.

elements.text: Identify the exact required output and its size.
Separate pre-existing input storage from memory allocated by the algorithm.
List temporary collections, recursion state, indices, and other working memory.
Report output space and auxiliary space separately.

feedback.reason: Space accounting begins with the contract, then separates input, result, and temporary state.
feedback.details: Without identifying the required output first, the same structure may be misclassified as either result storage or working memory. Separate reporting avoids double-counting.

items.prompt: Which structures should be counted as output space? Select all that apply.

options.text: A newly allocated array that is the required returned list of distinct values.
A frequency map that is returned as the required result.
A seen set discarded after producing a separate boolean result.
A normalized copy discarded after two inputs are compared.

feedback.reason: Output space consists of storage that directly represents the required result.
feedback.details: Returned collections are output when the contract requires their contents. Temporary sets and normalized copies are auxiliary when they are discarded after computing another result.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.temporary_seen_set: The seen set is working memory because it is discarded and the required output is only a boolean.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.temporary_normalized_copy: The copy supports the comparison but is not part of the returned result.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.returned_distinct_array: Its elements are exactly the required returned result.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.returned_frequency_map: The map itself is the requested output representation.

items.prompt: A sorted array is compacted in place so the valid result is the prefix [0, k), and the function returns k. Select the time, auxiliary-space, and additional output-space complexity.
constraints: The algorithm uses read and write indices.
No new result array is allocated.

feedback.reason: The algorithm scans the input once and stores the logical result inside existing input storage.
feedback.details: Time is O(n). A constant number of indices gives O(1) auxiliary space. Because the valid output reuses the input prefix and only k is returned separately, no growing additional output container is allocated.

items.prompt: A one-pass algorithm appends every retained element to a new result array. Which statement is correct?

options.text: The time may be O(n), while output space can still be O(k) for k retained elements.
One pass guarantees O(1) output and auxiliary space.
The result array is always auxiliary because it is created inside the loop.
Appending any number of elements necessarily makes time O(n²).

feedback.reason: Pass count determines traversal work, not the size of the required result.
feedback.details: A linear scan can construct an output whose size grows with the number of retained elements. The result array remains output space even though it is populated during execution.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_pass_constant_all_space: A single traversal can still materialize a large result.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.result_array_is_auxiliary: A structure's role is determined by the contract, not by where it is allocated.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.append_makes_quadratic: Appending does not inherently require scanning the existing output on every operation.

items.prompt: A function returns whether any duplicate exists and uses a set of all values seen so far. If there are d distinct values, which classification is correct?

options.text: O(1) output space and O(d) auxiliary space.
O(d) output space and O(1) auxiliary space.
Both the boolean and the set are output space.
Both are O(1) because set membership is expected O(1).

feedback.reason: The boolean is the required result, while the seen set is discarded working state.
feedback.details: The set may grow to d entries, so auxiliary space is O(d). Expected O(1) membership is an operation-cost statement, not a bound on total stored state.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.set_output_boolean_aux: The set is not returned, while the boolean is the required output.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.both_output: Temporary diagnostic state is not part of the output contract.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_constant: Constant expected lookup time does not imply a constant number of stored keys.

items.prompt: Which structures should be counted as auxiliary space? Select all that apply.

options.text: A seen set used to compute a boolean and discarded before return.
A copied array sorted only to help compare two inputs, then discarded.
The array explicitly required and returned by the function.
The caller-provided input array.

feedback.reason: Auxiliary space is extra working memory that is neither pre-existing input nor required result storage.
feedback.details: The temporary set and copied array support computation and are discarded. The returned array is output, while the original array is input storage.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.returned_output_array: Required result storage is output space rather than auxiliary memory.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.original_input_array: Memory supplied as input is not allocated as extra working state by the algorithm.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.temporary_seen_set: It exists only to support the computation of another result.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.temporary_copy_for_sort: The copied array is an additional working representation and is not returned.

items.prompt: Which statements correctly compare storage strategies for producing a filtered sequence? Select all that apply.

options.text: Writing the logical result into the input prefix can avoid a growing additional output container.
Appending directly to the returned array uses output space without requiring a separate temporary list.
Building a temporary list and then copying it into a returned array adds growing auxiliary space.
Implementations returning equal values must use the same auxiliary space.

feedback.reason: Output equivalence does not determine whether an implementation materializes temporary state.
feedback.details: In-place reuse, direct output construction, and temporary-then-copy strategies can produce the same values with different storage roles and peaks.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_values_same_aux: Equal results do not imply equal implementation memory.

omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.in_place_no_growing_result: The existing input prefix can serve as the logical result region.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.direct_output_no_temp: The required result can be built directly without a second collection.
omittedCorrectExplanationsByOptionId.temp_then_copy_more_aux: The temporary list is discarded working memory in addition to the returned output.

items.prompt: A function returns an empty array when every input value is filtered out, but its implementation first copies all n values into a temporary buffer. Which statement is precise for that input?

options.text: Output space is O(1) for the empty result, but auxiliary space is O(n) because of the temporary buffer.
Both output and auxiliary space are O(1) because the returned array is empty.
Output space is O(n) and auxiliary space is O(1).
Auxiliary space is O(n) only because the original input has n values.

feedback.reason: Output size and temporary allocation size can differ substantially on the same execution.
feedback.details: The required result contains no elements, but the implementation still allocates a full temporary copy. That copy is auxiliary because it is discarded rather than returned.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.all_constant: A small result does not erase large temporary allocations.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.linear_output_only: The n-element buffer is temporary, while the returned result is empty.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.input_counts_as_aux: The original input is not auxiliary; the newly allocated copy is.

items.prompt: A function emits each retained value to a caller-provided stream as soon as it is found and keeps only constant local state. Which statement is most precise?

options.text: It produces k output values while using O(1) auxiliary space without materializing all k values internally.
It has no output because no array is returned.
The emitted values count as O(k) auxiliary space inside the function.
Output size is O(1) because values are emitted one at a time.

feedback.reason: Streaming can avoid materializing the full result while the logical output still contains k values.
feedback.details: The function's internal working memory remains constant. Output size describes the amount produced, whereas materialized output storage depends on the interface and accounting convention.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_output_space: Producing values through a stream is still output behavior even without returning an array.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.aux_k: Values already handed to the output sink are not retained as the function's working memory.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.constant_output: Emitting one item at a time does not reduce the total number of output items.

items.prompt: A function compares two lowercase ASCII strings using two fixed 26-entry frequency arrays and returns one boolean. Select the time, auxiliary-space, and output-space complexity for input lengths n and m.
constraints: The alphabet size is fixed at 26.
The two counter arrays are not returned.

feedback.reason: Both inputs are scanned, fixed-domain counters are temporary, and the returned boolean is constant-size.
feedback.details: Time is O(n + m). Two 26-entry arrays are O(26), which is O(1) under a fixed alphabet. Because the required result is one boolean, output space is O(1).

items.prompt: A developer says, "The algorithm uses O(n) auxiliary space because it returns an array of n answers." What is the correct diagnosis?

options.text: The statement confuses required output storage with auxiliary working memory.
The statement is always correct because every returned value is auxiliary.
The only defect is confusing O(n) time with O(n) space.
The statement is wrong because all arrays use O(1) space.

feedback.reason: Required result storage and extra working storage must be reported separately.
feedback.details: The returned array may indeed occupy O(n) output space. Auxiliary space depends on what additional state is needed beyond that output.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.always_correct: Returned result storage is not automatically auxiliary.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.time_space_confusion: The quoted statement is specifically misclassifying memory roles, not traversal time.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.arrays_constant: An array's space scales with its number of stored elements.

items.prompt: Two implementations return the same k-element normalized sequence. Implementation A writes directly into the returned array. Implementation B first builds a k-element temporary list and then copies it into a separate returned array. Which comparison is correct?

options.text: Both use O(k) output space, but B uses O(k) more auxiliary space for the temporary list.
A uses more output space because it writes directly to the result.
They have identical output and auxiliary space because they return equal values.
B has no output space because its temporary list already contains the result.

feedback.reason: Equivalent outputs can be produced with different amounts of temporary working memory.
feedback.details: Each implementation returns a k-element array, so output space is O(k). B additionally retains a separate temporary k-element list before copying, which contributes O(k) auxiliary space.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.a_more_output: Both returned arrays have the same required size.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.same_all_space: Equal results do not imply equal temporary storage strategies.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.b_no_output: B still returns a separate k-element array; the temporary list does not replace the output contract.

items.prompt: A function scans n values, builds a frequency map with d entries, and returns that same map as the required result. Select its expected time, auxiliary-space, and output-space complexity.
constraints: No other growing collection is used.

feedback.reason: The growing map is the requested result, not temporary working memory.
feedback.details: The scan is expected O(n). Outside the returned map, only constant state is used, so auxiliary space is O(1), while output space is O(d).

items.prompt: A function returns one boolean indicating whether any duplicate exists and stores up to d distinct values in a temporary seen set. Select its expected time, auxiliary-space, and output-space complexity.
constraints: Hash membership and insertion are expected O(1).

feedback.reason: The seen set is discarded working memory, while the required result is constant-size.
feedback.details: The scan is expected O(n), the set may retain d keys, and the returned boolean occupies O(1) output space.
